mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index signal expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with rising INDPRO do not produce a 20-day forward positive return rate exceeding 55%, or the mean 20-day return in those months is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.0% indicates severe labor market slack that depresses wage growth and consumer spending power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the average 60-day return during these periods is not significantly below the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales confirm a synchronized recovery in both manufacturing and household demand, producing stronger positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days than either signal alone.
failure_condition: Periods where both INDPRO and RSAFS rise jointly do not produce a higher mean 20-day S&P 500 return than periods where only one rises (difference-of-means t-test p > 0.05), or the joint-signal positive hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, accelerating consumer price inflation reduces real debt burdens and indicates reflation, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this mechanism is inactive when the policy rate is elevated.
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS < 0.25% regimes, months with accelerating CPIAUCSL do not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than months with decelerating CPIAUCSL (t-test p > 0.05), or the conditional strategy underperforms buy-and-hold by more than 5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail and food services sales reflect accelerating household consumption expenditure, which raises current-quarter revenue expectations for consumer-facing firms and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with rising RSAFS do not produce a 5-day forward positive return rate exceeding 55%, or the mean 5-day return in rising-RSAFS months is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payroll employment exceeding 100 thousand persons signals contracting labor income and collapsing aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where PAYEMS drops by more than 100, S&P 500 20d forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the mean return in those months is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding economic output that supports broad-based earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with positive GDPC1 growth do not produce positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC of quarterly GDPC1 growth with 60-day returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units indicate a depressed residential construction sector that drags on cyclical earnings and construction-related employment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 600, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of weeks, or the mean 5-day return during triggered weeks is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
